# Impossible Powders

> Electrolyte powders specifically made for your training

## Overview

Impossible Powders is a line of purpose-built supplements. Each formula is designed for a specific use case, not a one-size-fits-all multivitamin in disguise.

Most electrolyte mixes are built for long cardio sessions, heavy on sodium and sugar. If you lift weights at the gym, that formula doesn't match what your body actually needs.

## Impossible Strength

Impossible Strength is the first product: an electrolyte mix optimised for resistance training and gym goers.

- Serving size: 7.5g
- Mix into: 500ml water
- Calories: <2 kcal
- Sugar: 0g
- Osmolality: Hypotonic (~135 mOsm/kg)
- EFSA compliant
- EU-authorised mineral forms

## Nutrition Facts (Per Serving)

- Sodium: 234 mg
- Potassium: 300 mg (15% NRV)
- Magnesium: 120 mg (32% NRV)
- Chloride: 91 mg (11% NRV)
- Zinc: 10 mg (100% NRV)
- Taurine: 1,000 mg

Recommended intake: 1-2 servings per day.

## Key Ingredients

- Tripotassium Citrate (790 mg) - citrate form for absorption and neuromuscular function
- Trisodium Citrate (745 mg) - improves mineral bioavailability and hydration
- Sodium Chloride (150 mg) - replaces sweat sodium without endurance-level doses
- Magnesium Malate (800 mg) - malate feeds the Krebs cycle for ATP production; 120 mg elemental
- Zinc Bisglycinate (33 mg) - highly bioavailable chelated form; 10 mg elemental
- L-Taurine (1,000 mg) - conditionally essential amino acid at research-relevant dose
- Citric Acid (600 mg) - tartness and pH correction
- Malic Acid (500 mg) - sustained sourness, masks mineral off-notes
- Stevia Reb-A 97% (50 mg) - non-caloric sweetness
- Erythritol (2,000 mg) - body, mouthfeel, zero calories
- Flavoring (800 mg) - spray-dried with maltodextrin carrier

Total serving weight: 7,468 mg

## How It Compares

Impossible Strength is the only formula that prioritises the minerals that matter most for resistance training: meaningful doses of chloride, magnesium, zinc and potassium in highly bioavailable forms. No empty sugar, no filler vitamins, no vitamin C mega-doses.

- Website: https://impossiblepowders.com
